text:
Once upon a sunny Tuesday afternoon on Long Island Sound, there was a small owl named Minerva. Minerva liked her quiet evenings best. She lived in a big old oak tree near the little village of Marshfield. Every evening after dusk, she would glide over the water, catching tiny fish with her sharp little feathers.

One evening, as Minerva drifted lazily through the cool fog, she heard a strange chirping. Curious, she fluttered down to a particularly quiet corner of her forest home. At the edge was a tiny hummingbird nesting a flower. The tiny bird couldn't move very fast—just the tiniest nibbles—and it seemed afraid.

Featuring a kind heart and a knack for soft voices, Minerva approached. "Hello there," she cooed. "Are you lost?"

The hummingbird fluttered closer. "No," it said sadly. "I'm just looking for my friend. She couldn't hop away because those thorns are too
